title: JLG Power Module Failure - Personality Range Error (9-9 / 99-5) | JLG Official
content_summary: JLG fault codes 9-9 or 99-5 on ES Scissors indicate Power Module Failure - Personality Range Error. The power module (PE) received an out-of-range value in its personality settings. Symptoms: machine fails to start, all functions unavailable. Cause: corrupted personality data in power module EPROM, or failed power module. Solution: (1) Cycle E-stop and key switch to attempt reset. (2) Connect JLG Analyzer (p/n 1001249695) to read full DTC. (3) If personality range error confirmed, reprogram or replace power module via JLG service. Models affected: ES Scissors series (1930ES, 2630ES, 3246ES, etc.).

title: JLG 3246 ES Scissor Lift Flash Code 2-5 Pothole Sensor Fault | JustAnswer
content_summary: JLG 3246 ES scissor lift fault code 2-5: Drive prevented due to pothole sensor issue. Code breakdown: first number (2) = platform control system, second number (5) = pothole/tilt fault. Cause: pothole bar not fully engaged or misaligned. Solution: (1) Inspect left and right pothole bars — both must be fully engaged and properly aligned. (2) Check pothole bar microswitches for continuity. (3) Inspect wiring harness to pothole switches for damage. (4) If bars are aligned but fault persists, check pothole switch adjustment. Reset: cycle E-stop after correcting pothole bar issue.

title: JLG T350 Flashing Code 9 - Service Required | Heavy Equipment Forums
content_summary: JLG T350 boom lift: Flash code 9 (single flash repeated) indicates machine requires service. Flash code 9 on JLG machines generally signals a system-level fault requiring diagnostic tool access. Procedure: (1) Release all E-stops. (2) Check footswitch engagement. (3) Verify machine is on level ground. (4) Connect JLG Analyzer for complete DTC reading — flash code alone insufficient for full diagnosis. (5) If analyzer unavailable, check battery voltage, inspect control module connections, and review error history if machine has display. JLG T350 uses 4WD articulating boom configuration; check swing/rotation sensor connections.

title: Control System Fault Codes - Genie Z-45/25 Service And Repair Manual Page 102 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Genie Z-45/25 articulated boom lift control system fault codes from official service and repair manual, page 102. The Z-45/25 uses Genie's standard fault code system with numeric codes covering electrical, hydraulic, sensor and control issues. Fault codes displayed on control module LED or display. General Genie fault code categories: E-codes for OIC (operational indicator), numbered DTC codes for device/circuit faults. Full service manual available on ManualsLib covers Z-45/25 hydraulics, electrical schematics, and complete troubleshooting procedures.

title: OIC and DTC Codes - Genie GS-2646AV Service And Repair Manual Page 92 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Genie GS-2646AV scissor lift Operation Indicator Codes (OIC) and Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTC) from service manual page 92. OIC codes: LL (off level — do not raise), PHS (pothole protection not deployed). DTC codes from Genie SmartLink Diagnostic System (GSDS) covering 5 categories: hydraulic, battery/power, user interface, sensors/switches, and controls. GS-2646AV uses same code system as GS-1932/GS-3246 with 272 DTC codes total. Full code chart begins at page 92 of the service manual.

title: Battery Charger Fault Codes and Flash Codes - JLG 1930ES Operator's Manual Page 58 | ManualsLib
content_summary: JLG 1930ES scissor lift battery charger fault codes and flash codes from operator's manual page 58. Battery charger LED flash patterns indicate: steady green = charging complete; flashing green = charging in progress; flashing red = charger fault. Common charger fault conditions: battery temperature out of range, battery voltage too low/high, charger overheating, bad cell detected. If charger fault: (1) Check battery water level. (2) Check battery connections for corrosion. (3) Verify AC input voltage. (4) Inspect charger connector. JLG 1930ES uses onboard 24V charger system.

title: OIC and DTC Fault Codes - Genie GS-1530/1532 Service And Repair Manual Page 180 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Genie GS-1530 and GS-1532 scissor lift complete OIC and DTC fault code table from service and repair manual page 180. Two code types: OIC (Operation Indicator Codes) for abnormal operating states, DTC (Diagnostic Trouble Codes) for device/circuit malfunctions detected by SmartLink GSDS. Key OIC codes: LL (off-level), PHS (pothole system). Key DTC categories: hydraulic coil faults, battery/power faults, user interface, sensor/switch faults, and control system. Full code chart with causes and corrective actions on page 180.

title: Genie GS-1930 Fault Codes Complete List and Fix Guide | Reliable Equipment Parts
content_summary: Complete Genie GS-1930/GS-1932 fault code list with descriptions and fixes. OIC codes: LL (off-level — lower platform, move to level surface), PHS (pothole switch fault). DTC codes: E1 (Emergency stop activated — check E-stop buttons), E4 (Low battery voltage — charge batteries, check connections), E6 (Tilt sensor activated — move to level ground, check tilt sensor), E12 (Platform-to-ground control communication loss — inspect CAN bus wiring), E18 (Height limit switch fault — check limit switch and wiring). Coil error codes: 52 (Forward coil), 53 (Reverse coil), 54 (Up coil), 55 (Down coil), 56 (Right coil), 57 (Left coil), 58 (Brake coil) — for coil faults: check coil resistance 20-30Ω, inspect wiring, check valve. Code 18: Pothole guard — check limit switches and harness. Code 68: Low voltage. Reset procedure: lower lift fully, turn off, use override mode, restart.

title: JLG Fault Code 211 - Power Cycle Required on Boom Lifts | GC Iron Support
content_summary: JLG boom lift fault code 211: Power cycle required. Appears on JLG articulating and telescopic boom lifts including 450AJ, 520AJ, 860SJ series. Code 211 indicates the control system requires a complete power cycle to clear a latched fault. Procedure: (1) Lower all boom functions to stowed/transport position. (2) Press and hold E-stop button. (3) Wait minimum 30 seconds for complete power discharge. (4) Release E-stop and restart with key. (5) If code 211 persists after power cycle, connect JLG Analyzer for underlying DTC. Common underlying causes: control module communication error, sensor data out of range, software fault latch.

title: DTC Introduction and Mast Sensor Default Code - JLG DSP M Operation Manual Page 93 | ManualsLib
content_summary: JLG DSP M mast-type vertical mast lift diagnostic trouble codes (DTC) from operation and safety manual page 93. Includes DTC system introduction and mast sensor default code documentation. JLG DSP M machines use coded diagnostic system: mast sensor faults display specific codes on the control panel. Mast sensor default code triggers when mast position sensor fails or reads out of range — machine disables lift function. Solutions: check mast sensor wiring, inspect sensor alignment on mast rail, verify sensor connector integrity. Full DTC list with corrective actions on page 93.

title: Comprehensive JLG Fault Codes Guide - JLG 660SJ and 860SJ | ManualsDump
content_summary: Comprehensive fault code guide for JLG telescopic boom lifts, specifically covering 660SJ, 860SJ, and E5002 models. JLG boom lift DTC format: two-part code (X-Y) where X identifies system group and Y identifies specific fault. Group 1 = ECM/engine, Group 2 = platform/basket, Group 3 = sensors, Group 4 = drive system, Group 5 = software/control. Code E5002: engine fault requiring service. Full analyzer-readable fault codes require JLG Analyzer (part 1001249695) connected to diagnostic port. For 860SJ: diagnostic port location is inside ground control box. Key service notes: after fault correction, always cycle power and verify code clears.

title: JLG Fault Codes List Ultimate Troubleshooting Guide | TractorDetailProblems
content_summary: Comprehensive JLG fault codes list covering all JLG MEWP models (scissor lifts, boom lifts, telehandlers). JLG DTC system overview: codes consist of two groups (system group-fault code), accessed via JLG Analyzer tool. Common fault groups: Group 1 (ECM/engine control), Group 2 (platform systems), Group 3 (sensors), Group 4 (drive/traction), Group 5 (control software). General troubleshooting sequence: (1) Note all active DTCs. (2) Start with highest group number first. (3) Check basics — battery, E-stops, footswitch. (4) Inspect wiring for corrosion and damage. (5) Use JLG Analyzer for live data monitoring. (6) Cycle power after each repair to verify code clears.

title: Analyzer Fault Code Listing - JLG 800S and 860SJ Service Manual Page 308 | ManualsDir
content_summary: JLG 800S and 860SJ telescopic boom lift complete analyzer fault code listing from service and maintenance manual, page 308. Contains all analyzer-readable DTC codes organized by group: Group 1 (ECM and engine control module codes), Group 2 (platform/basket controls), Group 3 (sensor faults — angle, pressure, height), Group 4 (drive system and traction), Group 5 (software and CAN bus). Example codes: DTC 001 (all OK), DTC 002 (ground mode OK), DTC 0010 (running at cutback). Requires JLG Analyzer (p/n 1001249695) connected to service port. Full DTC table with descriptions, probable causes, and corrective actions.

title: Default Fault Codes - Manitou Official Diagnostic Portal | Manitou Group
content_summary: Official Manitou Group diagnostic portal default fault codes page. Provides standard/default error codes for Manitou equipment including 180ATJ and other ATJ aerial work platforms, MT telehandlers, and industrial forklifts. This is Manitou's official diagnostic resource — codes listed here are manufacturer-validated. Before using full Manitou MDS diagnostic software, technicians can reference these default codes to identify common fault patterns. Covers engine codes, hydraulic faults, electrical system errors, and control module issues across Manitou product lines.

title: JLG 520AJ Service and Maintenance Manual 3121665 | Issuu
content_summary: JLG 520AJ articulating boom lift complete service and maintenance manual (document number 3121665). This is the full technical service reference for JLG 520AJ technicians. Contains: complete fault code listing with analyzer codes, hydraulic circuit diagrams, electrical schematics, CAN bus diagnostics, component replacement procedures, and calibration procedures. The 520AJ uses JLG's ADE (Advanced Drive Electronics) system with DTC codes readable via JLG Analyzer. Key systems covered: Deutz/Perkins engine fault codes, boom/swing/level hydraulics, platform weighing system, and two-speed drive. Manual number 3121665 is the standard factory service reference.
